records.ety: [OE. thogh, þah, AS. ðeh, ðǣh, ðēh; akin to OS. thōh, OFries. thach, D. & G. doch but, yet, OHG. doh but, yet though, Icel. þō yet, nevertheless, Sw. dock, Dan. dog, Goth. þuh, þu, than, or, yet; of uncertain origin. √184.]

defs: Granting, admitting, or supposing that; notwithstanding that; if.
quotes: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him.
Not that I so affirm, though so it seem.
In the vine were three branches; and it was as though it budded.
notes: ☞ It is compounded with all in although. See Although.

defs: However; nevertheless; notwithstanding; -- used in familiar language, and in the middle or at the end of a sentence.
quotes: I would not be as sick though for his place.
A good cause would do well, though.
